user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction that failed due to the Defendant's default. The suit pleads total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act and claims a refund with interest and costs.

slots.value: Recovery of advance with interest and costs

missing_fields: Date of accrual of cause of action
assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on valuation or cause of action
Suit is filed within the limitation period from the date of failure of consideration

In Ratnavarma Raja v. Vimla, 83 the Supreme Court has observed that, whether proper court fee has been paid 
or not, is a issue between the plaintiff and the state and that, the defendant has no right to question it in a 
revision. In view of this pronouncement, some of the decisions noted above might require reconsideration. 84 
The Allahabad High Court has held that a decision in the trial of a suit as to the amount of court fee, is not an 
independent proceeding and, therefore, not open to revision. 85 The Oudh High Court has held that an order 
demanding deficit court fee from a plaintiff is not open to revision.86

application for restoration was not maintainable.16
But the second remedy, that is, the remedy provided by this rule, can only be availed of by a plaintiff who does not 
appear at the hearing and the suit is dismissed for default of appearance under r 8 above. The remedy given by this 
rule is not open to a plaintiff whose suit is dismissed on any ground other than default of appearance. Hence, if a 
plaintiffs suit is dismissed on his failure to establish his case by reason of non-attendance of his witnesses 17 or for 
want of evidence, 18 the dismissal is not one under r 8, and he cannot, therefore, avail himself of the remedy 
provided by this rule.

he is entitled to apply for a review under O 47, r 1, after the expiration of that period. The Patna High Court has

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint under Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid as advance due to total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
draft_artifacts.text:
Plaint filed under Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 in the District Court at {{DISTRICT_COURT_CITY}} for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only) paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to the Defendant's default. The Plaintiff pleads total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 and seeks refund of the advance amount together with interest and costs. 

2. Parties: Plaintiff – {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (Plaintiff); Defendant – {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} (Defendant). Addresses and other particulars of the parties are as set out in the blank below and may be supplied as and when available by the party. 

3. Jurisdiction and Venue: The suit is filed in the District Court, {{DISTRICT_COURT_CITY}}, which has jurisdiction to entertain suits of the nature and value aforesaid. The value of the subject-matter of the suit falls within the pecuniary jurisdiction of the District Court as per the Court’s local rules. 

4. Facts and Cause of Action (Material Facts):  
   (a) On {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only) as an advance to the Defendant for a business transaction. 
   (b) The said transaction failed on account of the Defendant's default, resulting in a total failure of consideration for the payment made. 
   (c) By reason of the above, the Plaintiff is entitled to recover the said amount along with interest and costs, on the basis of Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. 
   (d) The cause of action arose on the date of the failure of the consideration and the right to sue accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. The cause of action is continuing in nature as the Defendant has not refunded the amount despite due demand and the obligation to repay remains outstanding. 

5. Accrual and Continuity of Cause of Action: 
   (a) Accrual event: The date on which the consideration failed is {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. 
   (b) Accrual of right to sue: The right to sue accrued on the date of failure of consideration, i.e., {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. 
   (c) Continuing nature: The present cause of action is of a continuing nature until the refund is made in full or until legally completed relief is granted. 

6. Reliefs prayed: 
   (i) A decree for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh) with interest thereon from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization; 
   (ii) Costs of this suit; 
   (iii) Such other and further reliefs as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper. 

7. Interest: The Plaintiff prays for interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable, with pre-suit interest from the accrual date to filing, pendente lite interest during the subsistence of the suit, and future interest from the date of decree to date of actual realization. (Details to be provided at filing if the rate is specified or conditional.) 

8. Valuation for Court Fee and Subject to Court Fee: The suit value is Rs. 20,00,000/-. The Court Fee payable is to be calculated as per applicable rates; {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is provided as a placeholder for the Court Fee to be verified at the time of filing. 

9. Limitation (Mandatory): The present suit is within limitation under Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 (For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails) – limitation period three years – accrual on the date of failure; the suit is therefore within time. The accrual date for the limitation computation is {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. The present suit is a continuing cause of action as explained above.  

10. Verification: I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff/authorized representative, do hereby verify and state on oath that the contents of the above plaint are true to my knowledge and belief and that I have not suppressed any material fact. 

11. List of Documents / Annexures: The Plaintiff prays that no documents be treated as annexures unless and until required by the Court; if any, Exhibit labels shall be provided in the order of filing. 

12. Place, Date and Verification: The plaint is signed at {{PLACE}} on this day {{DATE}}.

Notes: Placeholder tokens used: {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} (Date of payment of advance); {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} (Date of accrual of cause of action); {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (Plaintiff’s name); {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} (Defendant’s name); {{DISTRICT_COURT_CITY}} (District Court city); {{PLACE}} (Place of filing); {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (Court fee amount to be verified at filing). The placeholders were inserted to maintain filing readiness given missing factual specifics in the intake. 

Citations used: ["rag-doc-1", "rag-doc-2"]

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint under Order VII Rule 1 CPC for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Advance Paid) due to Total Failure of Consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_COURT_CITY}}

Civil Suit No. _____ of 20__

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o {{PLAINTIFF_GUARDIAN_NAME}}, Age: __ years,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
(also at/working at: {{PLAINTIFF_ALT_ADDRESS}}, if any)
                                                                                      ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o {{DEFENDANT_GUARDIAN_NAME}}, Age: __ years,
Residing at / Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},
                                                                                      ...Defendant

PLAINT FILED UNDER ORDER VII RULE 1 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF RS. 20,00,000/- (RUPEES TWENTY LAKH ONLY) PAID AS ADVANCE, ON ACCOUNT OF TOTAL FAILURE OF CONSIDERATION UNDER SECTION 65 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872, WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

1. Particulars of the Suit:
   (a) The Plaintiff seeks a decree for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only) paid as advance to the Defendant for a business transaction, which failed due to the Defendant’s default, resulting in total failure of consideration.
   (b) The Plaintiff pleads refund/restoration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 and seeks interest and costs.

2. Parties:
   (a) Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (details as in cause title above).
   (b) Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} (details as in cause title above).

3. Jurisdiction (Territorial and Pecuniary):
   (a) This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction because (choose/apply as applicable):
       (i) the Defendant resides/carries on business/works for gain within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS_WITHIN_JURISDICTION}}; and/or
       (ii) the cause of action arose wholly/partly within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court inasmuch as the advance amount was paid at {{PLACE_OF_PAYMENT}} and/or the business transaction was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}} and/or the Defendant committed default/failure at {{PLACE_OF_DEFAULT}}.
   (b) This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only), which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

4. Facts Constituting Cause of Action (Material Facts):
   (a) On {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}, the Plaintiff paid to the Defendant a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only) as advance in connection with a proposed business transaction, namely: {{BRIEF_DESCRIPTION_OF_TRANSACTION}}.
   (b) The payment was made towards consideration for the Defendant’s performance/obligation under the said transaction, namely: {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATION}}.
   (c) The Defendant failed to perform and/or committed default, due to which the said business transaction failed, resulting in total failure of consideration in respect of the advance paid by the Plaintiff.
   (d) The consideration is stated to have failed on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} (date of failure of consideration).
   (e) The Plaintiff requested/demanded refund from the Defendant on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}}. Despite requests and demands, the Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the said amount.
   (f) By virtue of Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, upon total failure of consideration, the Defendant is bound to restore/refund the advantage received, i.e., Rs. 20,00,000/-, to the Plaintiff.

5. Cause of Action (Accrual and Continuity):
   (a) The cause of action first arose on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when the consideration for which the advance was paid failed due to the Defendant’s default and the Plaintiff’s right to seek restoration/refund matured.
   (b) The cause of action further subsists/continues in the sense that the Defendant continues to withhold/refuse refund of the Plaintiff’s money despite demands, and the Plaintiff continues to suffer the consequences of such non-refund, until the amount is refunded or the claim is satisfied by decree.
   (c) For the purposes of limitation, the Plaintiff relies on accrual from the date of failure of consideration, i.e., {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}.

6. Limitation:
   The present suit is within limitation. The claim is for money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails, and is governed by Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date of the failure. The date of failure is {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} and the suit is filed within the prescribed period.

7. Valuation and Court Fee:
   (a) For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only).
   (b) The requisite court fee is payable on the said suit valuation as per the applicable Court Fees law/rules in force in the State of {{STATE}} and is tentatively computed as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be verified at filing and paid accordingly).

8. Interest:
   The Plaintiff prays for interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE_PCT}}% per annum (or such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit and proper) from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization, including:
   (a) pre-suit interest (as applicable) up to the date of filing;
   (b) pendente lite interest during the pendency of the suit; and
   (c) future interest from the date of decree till realization.

9. Reliefs / Prayer:
   In the above facts and circumstances, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:
   (i) pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only);
   (ii) award interest on the said sum at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE_PCT}}% per annum (or such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit) from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization;
   (iii) award costs of the suit; and
   (iv) grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

10. List of Documents (as available; Plaintiff craves leave to file additional documents):
   (a) Proof of payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} ({{MODE_OF_PAYMENT}})  e.g., bank statement/UTR/receipt  {{PAYMENT_PROOF_DETAILS}};
   (b) Writing/communication evidencing the business transaction and obligations (agreement/offer/terms/messages/emails)  {{TRANSACTION_DOCUMENT_DETAILS}};
   (c) Demand/request for refund dated {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} and any reply/communications  {{DEMAND_DOCUMENT_DETAILS}};
   (d) Any other documents as may be filed at the time of evidence.

11. Verification (Order VI Rule 15 CPC):
   I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 are true and correct to my personal knowledge, and the contents of paragraphs 10 and 11 are true on the basis of records/information believed to be true and legal advice received, and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{DATE}}.


_________________________
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
Plaintiff

blocking_issues.issue: Territorial jurisdiction and jurisdictional facts are not pleaded (where Defendant resides/carries on business and/or where cause of action arose). A generic statement that the District Court has jurisdiction may lead to return of plaint for presentation before proper court.
blocking_issues.fix: Add specific jurisdictional averments with placeholders (e.g., Defendant’s residence/place of business; place of payment; place where transaction was to be performed; place where breach/failure occurred) and assert that cause of action arose wholly/partly within the court’s territorial jurisdiction.

blocking_issues.issue: Verification clause is not compliant with Order VI Rule 15 CPC practice because it does not specify which statements are verified on personal knowledge and which are based on information/legal advice.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace verification with an Order VI Rule 15-style verification specifying paragraph ranges verified on knowledge vs information and include signature, place and date.

blocking_issues.issue: Draft includes non-filing metadata and drafting notes inside the plaint (e.g., “Notes: Placeholder tokens used…”, “Citations used: [\"rag-doc-1\", …]”, and language about “blank below”). This can trigger filing/registry objections and is not part of a plaint.
blocking_issues.fix: Remove all drafting notes/metadata from the plaint body; keep placeholders in-text where necessary without explanatory notes; present party particulars as standard placeholders instead of referring to blanks; remove RAG chunk IDs from the plaint text.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Interest relief is pleaded without indicating a claimed rate; leaving it entirely to the court may reduce clarity of the monetary claim.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Add a placeholder rate (e.g., {{INTEREST_RATE_PCT}}% p.a.) or plead a specific rate based on contract/trade usage, while retaining the alternative prayer “as deemed fit”.

non_blocking_issues.issue: List of Documents section is drafted as “no annexures unless required”. Many courts/registries expect a concrete list of documents relied upon (even if originals to be produced later).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Provide a basic list with placeholders (agreement/offer, payment proof, correspondence/demand) and reserve right to file additional documents.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The suit is filed in the District Court, {{DISTRICT_COURT_CITY}}, which has jurisdiction to entertain suits of the nature and value aforesaid… as per the Court’s local rules.”
unsupported_statements.reason: No jurisdictional facts are pleaded (where Defendant resides/carries on business; where payment/transaction/breach occurred). A bare conclusion on jurisdiction is unsupported without those facts.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The cause of action is continuing in nature… until the refund is made in full…” (as used to support limitation/continuity).
unsupported_statements.reason: Non-refund after a one-time failure of consideration does not automatically constitute a “continuing cause of action” for limitation purposes; needs careful qualification so as not to misstate limitation accrual.

final_artifacts.title: Plaint under Order VII Rule 1 CPC for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Advance Paid) due to Total Failure of Consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
final_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_COURT_CITY}}

Civil Suit No. _____ of 20__

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o {{PLAINTIFF_GUARDIAN_NAME}}, Age: __ years,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
(also at/working at: {{PLAINTIFF_ALT_ADDRESS}}, if any)
                                                                                      ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o {{DEFENDANT_GUARDIAN_NAME}}, Age: __ years,
Residing at / Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},
                                                                                      ...Defendant

PLAINT FILED UNDER ORDER VII RULE 1 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF RS. 20,00,000/- (RUPEES TWENTY LAKH ONLY) PAID AS ADVANCE, ON ACCOUNT OF TOTAL FAILURE OF CONSIDERATION UNDER SECTION 65 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872, WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

1. Particulars of the Suit:
   (a) The Plaintiff seeks a decree for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only) paid as advance to the Defendant for a business transaction, which failed due to the Defendant’s default, resulting in total failure of consideration.
   (b) The Plaintiff pleads refund/restoration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 and seeks interest and costs.

2. Parties:
   (a) Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (details as in cause title above).
   (b) Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} (details as in cause title above).

3. Jurisdiction (Territorial and Pecuniary):
   (a) This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction because (choose/apply as applicable):
       (i) the Defendant resides/carries on business/works for gain within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS_WITHIN_JURISDICTION}}; and/or
       (ii) the cause of action arose wholly/partly within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court inasmuch as the advance amount was paid at {{PLACE_OF_PAYMENT}} and/or the business transaction was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}} and/or the Defendant committed default/failure at {{PLACE_OF_DEFAULT}}.
   (b) This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only), which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

4. Facts Constituting Cause of Action (Material Facts):
   (a) On {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}, the Plaintiff paid to the Defendant a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only) as advance in connection with a proposed business transaction, namely: {{BRIEF_DESCRIPTION_OF_TRANSACTION}}.
   (b) The payment was made towards consideration for the Defendant’s performance/obligation under the said transaction, namely: {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATION}}.
   (c) The Defendant failed to perform and/or committed default, due to which the said business transaction failed, resulting in total failure of consideration in respect of the advance paid by the Plaintiff.
   (d) The consideration is stated to have failed on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} (date of failure of consideration).
   (e) The Plaintiff requested/demanded refund from the Defendant on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}}. Despite requests and demands, the Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the said amount.
   (f) By virtue of Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, upon total failure of consideration, the Defendant is bound to restore/refund the advantage received, i.e., Rs. 20,00,000/-, to the Plaintiff.

5. Cause of Action (Accrual and Continuity):
   (a) The cause of action first arose on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when the consideration for which the advance was paid failed due to the Defendant’s default and the Plaintiff’s right to seek restoration/refund matured.
   (b) The cause of action further subsists/continues in the sense that the Defendant continues to withhold/refuse refund of the Plaintiff’s money despite demands, and the Plaintiff continues to suffer the consequences of such non-refund, until the amount is refunded or the claim is satisfied by decree.
   (c) For the purposes of limitation, the Plaintiff relies on accrual from the date of failure of consideration, i.e., {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}.

6. Limitation:
   The present suit is within limitation. The claim is for money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails, and is governed by Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date of the failure. The date of failure is {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} and the suit is filed within the prescribed period.

7. Valuation and Court Fee:
   (a) For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only).
   (b) The requisite court fee is payable on the said suit valuation as per the applicable Court Fees law/rules in force in the State of {{STATE}} and is tentatively computed as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be verified at filing and paid accordingly).

8. Interest:
   The Plaintiff prays for interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE_PCT}}% per annum (or such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit and proper) from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization, including:
   (a) pre-suit interest (as applicable) up to the date of filing;
   (b) pendente lite interest during the pendency of the suit; and
   (c) future interest from the date of decree till realization.

9. Reliefs / Prayer:
   In the above facts and circumstances, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:
   (i) pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh only);
   (ii) award interest on the said sum at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE_PCT}}% per annum (or such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit) from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization;
   (iii) award costs of the suit; and
   (iv) grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

10. List of Documents (as available; Plaintiff craves leave to file additional documents):
   (a) Proof of payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} ({{MODE_OF_PAYMENT}})  e.g., bank statement/UTR/receipt  {{PAYMENT_PROOF_DETAILS}};
   (b) Writing/communication evidencing the business transaction and obligations (agreement/offer/terms/messages/emails)  {{TRANSACTION_DOCUMENT_DETAILS}};
   (c) Demand/request for refund dated {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} and any reply/communications  {{DEMAND_DOCUMENT_DETAILS}};
   (d) Any other documents as may be filed at the time of evidence.

11. Verification (Order VI Rule 15 CPC):
   I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 are true and correct to my personal knowledge, and the contents of paragraphs 10 and 11 are true on the basis of records/information believed to be true and legal advice received, and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{DATE}}.


_________________________
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
Plaintiff
